mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals immediate labor market tightening, which reduces recession risk premiums and drives short-term risk-on equity flows, boosting S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 5-day forward returns following a >0.2pp UNRATE drop are statistically indistinguishable from flat periods (p > 0.10) or consistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances (RSAFS) combined with increasing housing starts (HOUST) indicate synchronized household demand expansion. This dual signal confirms economic momentum, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The signal fails if SPX 20-day returns are negative or flat in >60% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST show simultaneous month-over-month increases.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects capacity utilization rebound and supply chain normalization. This supply-side improvement expands profit margins, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO growth during identified recovery phases, or if supply shocks cause INDPRO rises to coincide with equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating headline inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeding a 0.5% monthly threshold forces market pricing of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening. This compression of real yields and higher discount rates depresses equity valuations, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 20-day forward returns remain positive or neutral in >50% of instances where CPIAUCSL monthly growth exceeds 0.5%, indicating markets decouple from inflation-tightening transmission.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) coinciding with a declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirms labor market tightness and wage pressure. This dual confirmation reduces recession fears and drives short-term equity risk premiums lower, boosting S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months where PAYEMS exceeds 150k and UNRATE declines simultaneously, indicating the labor signal is priced in or interpreted as inflationary.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) expansion directly increases forward corporate earnings expectations. This fundamental demand signal mechanically supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 60-day forward returns show no positive relationship with GDPC1 growth rates over a 5-year rolling window, or if GDP contractions fail to precede equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle monetary tightening regime, elevated policy rates (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) combined with slowing demand indicators suppress equity valuations. The high cost of capital compresses multiple expansion, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, indicating equity markets have fully priced in the tightening cycle or are driven by liquidity rather than rates.
